title: Mind in Motion Young Adults Walking Over Uneven Terrain
description: This dataset comprises multimodal neurophysiological and biomechanical recordings from 32 young adults during walking over uneven terrain at varying speeds, plus a seated rest baseline condition. Data include high-density dual-layer electroencephalography (EEG), neck electromyography (EMG), inertial measurement unit (IMU) acceleration, ground reaction forces, and structural MRI-derived head models. Participants completed two trials per walking condition lasting three minutes each, plus a three-minute seated rest baseline, enabling investigation of neural and muscular control mechanisms during dynamic locomotion.
methods_description: Data collection involved 32 participants performing walking trials over uneven terrain at different speeds, with two repetitions per condition and a three-minute seated rest baseline condition. High-density dual-layer EEG, neck EMG, IMU acceleration, and ground reaction forces were recorded simultaneously. Digitized electrode locations were obtained for each participant, and T1 structural MR images were acquired for head model construction.
